evidence: All Java files follow standard camelCase for methods and PascalCase for classes — no snake_case outliers
Package names use underscore: pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch — non-standard for Java (convention is all-lowercase without underscores), but this is consistent across all packages
Handler classes follow a consistent pattern: ExplorationHandlers and SketchHandlers are both package-private, constructor-injected, and delegate to HttpApiUtils

Model types use two distinct strategies: records (DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, AdjectiveProfile, Edge, ExploreOptions, QueryResults.*) and traditional classes (ExplorationResult, ComparisonResult) — rationale is documented in Javadoc for each traditional class

Constant naming is inconsistent across similar concepts: SemanticFieldExplorer uses 'NOUN_CQL_CONSTRAINT' while AdjectiveCollocateRanker uses 'ADJECTIVE_PATTERN' — both are CQL constraint strings but named with different conventions
Private record ExploreParams in ExplorationHandlers vs public record ExploreOptions in model package — both are option-bag records for the same exploration layer; naming diverges (Params vs Options)

BlackLab prefix appears in both package path (indexer/blacklab/) and class names (BlackLabQueryExecutor, BlackLabConllUIndexer, BlackLabSnippetParser) — the prefix is redundant in classes already within the blacklab subpackage but consistent with classes in the query package where BlackLab specifics must be distinguished
Accessor method prefix: ExplorationResult and ComparisonResult use 'get' prefix (getSeed(), getNouns()), while all record types omit the prefix — documented as intentional, but the asymmetry is real

strengths: Consistent Java naming conventions (camelCase methods, PascalCase classes) throughout all 30+ source files
The two-strategy model type system (records vs traditional classes) is documented with explicit rationale, making it a convention rather than an inconsistency
Handler class structure is uniform across ExplorationHandlers and SketchHandlers
convention_outlier.issue_character: Issues are minor naming drift for closely related concepts — particularly constant names for CQL pattern strings and the Params/Options divergence — rather than structural outliers.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: The codebase is broadly convention-consistent; no file is a true style island. The identifiable drift (NOUN_CQL_CONSTRAINT vs ADJECTIVE_PATTERN, ExploreParams vs ExploreOptions) is localized and low-friction. This is solid quality with minor naming polish needed — a 77 reflects the slight friction without penalizing what is an otherwise clean convention landscape.

issues.summary: CQL constraint constants use inconsistent naming: NOUN_CQL_CONSTRAINT vs ADJECTIVE_PATTERN for the same concept
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer: private static final String NOUN_CQL_CONSTRAINT = "[xpos=\"NN.*\"]"
AdjectiveCollocateRanker: private static final String ADJECTIVE_PATTERN = "[xpos=\"JJ.*\"]"
Both are CQL constraint strings used as the simplePattern argument to findCollocations(); the naming should reflect the same abstraction level
issues.suggestion: Standardize to either the _PATTERN or _CQL_CONSTRAINT suffix (prefer _PATTERN as it is shorter and already used in one place), or move both constants to a shared constants class in utils/config.

issues.summary: ExploreParams (private record in ExplorationHandlers) and ExploreOptions (public record in model) are sibling option-bag types with inconsistent naming
evidence: ExplorationHandlers: private record ExploreParams(int topCollocates, int minShared, double minLogDice, int nounsPerSeed) {}
model/ExploreOptions.java: public record ExploreOptions(int topCollocates, int nounsPerSeed, double minLogDice, int minShared, boolean includeExamples)
Both carry exploration tuning parameters; one is named Params, the other Options, with no documented rationale for the difference
issues.suggestion: Rename ExploreParams to ExploreRequestParams (to emphasize it is HTTP-layer request parsing) or consolidate if the parameter sets are similar enough to share a type.

root_causes: Constants defined locally in each class rather than in a shared constants location
Internal HTTP-layer record named independently of the existing model record
possible_false_positives: get-prefix vs no-prefix accessor asymmetry — this is documented as intentional and should not be flagged
